channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Federal prosecutors file new indictment against ex-Louisville police officers
item.description: Federal prosecutors filed a new indictment against two former Louisville officers accused of falsifying a warrant that led police to Breonna Taylor’s door before they fatally shot her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Texas man is executed for fatally stabbing twin teenage girls in 1989
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man convicted of fatally stabbing twin 16-year-old girls more than three decades ago was executed on Tuesday evening. Garcia Glenn White was pronounced dead at 6:56 p.m. CDT following a chemical injection at the state penitentiary in Huntsville.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KREX 5

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs accused of sexual misconduct by 120 people, attorney says
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Habit Burger & Grill trolls rival In-N-Out
item.description: Habit Burger & Grill isn’t letting anyone, especially In-N-Out Burger, forget that it has the best fast-food burger in town—at least according to one survey.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Hair found in truck helps police identify suspect in 1995 disappearance of Morgan Nick, 6
item.description: ALMA, Ark. (AP) — Hair found in a truck once owned by an Arkansas man who died in prison more than two decades ago was the clue investigators needed to connect the dead inmate to the disappearance of a 6-year-old girl who went missing in 1995.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DEMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: John Amos, patriarch on 'Good Times' and an Emmy nominee for the blockbuster 'Roots,' dies at 84
item.description: John Amos, who starred as the family patriarch on the hit 1970s sitcom “Good Times” and earned an Emmy nomination for his role in the seminal 1977 miniseries “Roots,” has died.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch live: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 vice presidential debate
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz will meet Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KRON4

item.title: Morgan Wallen donates $500,000 for Hurricane Helene relief
item.description: East Tennessee native Morgan Wallen is giving a half million dollars towards relief efforts after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eric Adams defense hinges on Supreme Court ruling in heartland trucking case
item.description: An Indiana mayor's successful Supreme Court battle against Justice Department overreach could boost New York City Mayor Eric Adams as he fights his own charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search crews with cadaver dogs wade through muck of communities ‘wiped off the map’ by Helene
item.description: Rescuers are scouring the mountains of western North Carolina for anyone still unaccounted for since Hurricane Helene’s remnants caused catastrophic damage across the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With New York City aqueduct in repair, mayor promises water will remain safe and tasty
item.description: New York City officials assured residents that water flowing from their faucets will continue to be perfectly safe — even if it might taste a bit different — as they announced plans Monday to address massive leaks in the supply system by shutting down part of a main aqueduct for the winter.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Donate today to help Red Cross efforts across 10 states
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Days after Hurricane Helene's deadly force tore a path north from the Gulf Coast, many Americans remained without power, water and other basic necessities. The hurricane's destructive path necessitated a Red Cross response spanning 10 states, according to the non-profit.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: Maya Rudolph as Harris and Dana Carvey as Biden open the 50th season of 'Saturday Night Live'
item.description: “Saturday Night Live” began its 50th season with a parade of former co-stars, including Maya Rudolph as Vice President Kamala Harris, Andy Samberg as her husband Doug Emhoff and Dana Carvey as President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: John Ashton of 'Beverly Hills Cop' dies at 76
item.description: In a career that spanned more than 50 years, Ashton was a regular face across TV series and films, including "Midnight Run,” “Little Big League” and “Gone Baby Gone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Henry runs for 199 yards and Jackson has 3 TDs as Ravens rout previously unbeaten Bills 35-10
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Derrick Henry was off and running from the first time he touched the ball. There was no way the Buffalo Bills were stopping the Baltimore Ravens after that.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 California firefighters hurt in crash while heading to help Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: "These first responders put their own lives on the line every day, here at home and around the country when called," said San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom vetoed a landmark bill aimed at establishing first-in-the-nation safety measures for large artificial intelligence models Sunday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kris Kristofferson, country music superstar and actor, dies at 88
item.description: Kris Kristofferson, a Rhodes scholar with a deft writing style and rough charisma who became a country music superstar and A-list Hollywood actor, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man killed, sister pistol-whipped while delivering pizza in Illinois
item.description: An Illinois family is pleading for answers after a young man was killed and his sister pistol whipped while out together on a pizza delivery.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Videos show severe flooding, damage from Helene
item.description: As the worst of Helene has moved on, many are taking to social media to share videos of the severe flooding and damage.
item.dc:creator: Mike Costanza, WFLA, NBC

item.title: This Halloween decoration can be dangerous for deer, wildlife officials warn
item.description: Fall is in full swing and many have already decked out their properties with Halloween decorations, but wildlife experts say that one decoration in particular can be harmful to deer in the area.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, ABC 4

item.title: Awareness of 'Latinx' increases among US Latinos, and 'Latine' emerges as an alternative
item.description: “Latino” and “Hispanic” have long been the most prominent terms used to describe people in the U.S. with roots in Latin America and Spain.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: VIDEO: Donald Trump tosses chicken tenders into crowd at college football game
item.description: Former President Donald Trump surprised college students on Saturday in Alabama with an unusual gift for football fans.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals stretch 4 decades for a prisoner convicted on little police evidence
item.description: NEW KENSINGTON, Pa. (AP) — The four men who put Steve Szarewicz away for murder all changed their stories at one time or another, yet Szarewicz still sits behind bars. That's where he has been for almost 43 years.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Conservative Christians were skeptical of mail-in ballots. Now they are gathering them in churches
item.description: MENIFEE, California (AP) — With Election Day just a few weeks away, longtime church members Lucky Hartunian and Janie Booth sat outside the Revival Christian Fellowship's sanctuary in Menifee, California, inviting congregants to register to vote.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 64 dead and millions without power after Helene's deadly march across the Southeast
item.description: PERRY, Fla. (AP) — Massive rains from powerful Hurricane Helene left people stranded, without shelter and awaiting rescue Saturday, as the cleanup began from a tempest that killed at least 64 people, caused widespread destruction across the U.S. Southeast and left millions without power.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN SMITH, KATE PAYNE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: High-speed train project chugs ahead as $3 billion in Nevada funding gets OK
item.description: The high-speed train between Las Vegas and Southern California took a big step forward this week when $3 billion in funding got an important signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, Fox 5

item.title: Footage of motorcade racing JFK to the hospital after he was shot sells for $137,500 at auction
item.description: Newly emerged film footage of President John F. Kennedy's motorcade speeding down a Dallas freeway toward a hospital after he was fatally wounded sold at auction Saturday for $137,500.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah mom raises over $750k for own funeral after terminal cancer diagnosis
item.description: A Utah mother who has been fighting for life after being diagnosed with a rare terminal cancer has now been given three months to live, and she's decided to spend it with her kids.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, Fox 5

item.title: 2 passengers injured on United flight after plane tries to avoid collision
item.description: Two United Airlines passengers were sent to the hospital after they were injured during the descent of a San Francisco-bound flight on Thursday, the United team and the Federal Aviation Administration told KRON4.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, WGN 9
